ABS Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction in the electronically controlled brake system is detected. This circuit consists of the brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) and seat belt control ECU. Signals are sent from the brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) using the CAN communication. HINT This circuit uses CAN communication. Therefore, be sure to check that the CAN communication system is normal before performing inspection See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC OUTPUT (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Pre-Collision System. (b) Recheck for DTCs. OK: CAN communication system DTC is not output. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With OK -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: Communication Error from Driving Support ECU to VSC ECU This DTC is stored when CAN communication between the driving support ECU and brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) is interrupted. This circuit consists of the driving support ECU, brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) and seat belt control ECU. DTC B2085 is stored when the seat belt control ECU detects an interruption in CAN communication between the driving support ECU and brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU). HINT This circuit uses CAN communication. Therefore, be sure to check that the CAN communication system is normal before performing inspection See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Driving Support System Malfunction The seat belt control ECU receives collision information from the driving support ECU and brake booster with master cylinder (skid control ECU) via CAN communication, and tightens the seat belt by operating the motor inside the front seat outer belt. When the driving support ECU detects a malfunction in the dynamic radar cruise control system and pre-collision system, the malfunction information is sent to the seat belt control ECU, which outputs this DTC. When DTC B2090 is detected, other DTCs are also stored by the driving support ECU (dynamic radar cruise control system and pre-collision system). Also, if the lane recognition camera sensor assembly beam axis adjustment is not performed after installing the lane recognition camera sensor assembly,

dtcs.description: Ignition Hold Monitor Malfunction This DTC is stored when a problem such as an open in the AM2 fuse, an open or short in the wire harness between the fuse and power management control ECU, a short in the IG output circuit inside the power management control ECU, a short between the power management control ECU and relay, or a short in the relay is detected. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) (a) Disconnect the L5 and L6 connectors from the power management control ECU. dtcs.description: ACC Monitor Malfunction This DTC is stored when there is a problem in the ACC output circuit. The ACC circuit is the circuit that goes from inside the power management control ECU to the ACC relay. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Smart Key System (for Start Function). (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) After 330 seconds have elapsed, check if the DTC has been set again. OK:

dtcs.description: Vehicle Speed Signal Malfunction The power management control ECU receives vehicle speed information using 2 methods. It receives a speed signal from the meter ECU via a direct line, and a from the skid control ECU via CAN. If the information sent using these 2 methods is inconsistent, this DTC will be stored. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE s/CAN Communication System OK -- Continue to next step.

steps.instruction: CHECK CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Check if CAN communication DTCs are output. HINT If any DTCs for CAN communication system malfunction are output, inspect those DTCs first. OK: CAN communication DTC is not output. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (DIAGNOSTIC TROUBLE CODE CHART)  See: Diagnostic Trouble Code

CHECK SPEEDOMETER OPERATION (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Combination Meter / Data List. (e) Check the Data List for proper functioning of the vehicle speed signal. Combination Meter OK: Vehicle speed displayed on the Techstream is almost the same as the actual vehicle speed measured using a speedometer tester (calibrated chassis dynamometer). NG --  GO TO METER / GAUGE SYSTEM (Speedometer Malfunction)  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Speedometer Malfunction OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (a) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Power Source Control / Data List. (b) Read the Data List according to the display on the Techstream. Power Source Control OK: Stop (vehicle is stopped) and Run (vehicle is running) appear on the screen. NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) OK --  REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU)   See: B2271 NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU - BODY GROUND)   See: B2271 NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU - BODY GROUND) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) (a) Disconnect the L27 connector from the combination meter assembly. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY - NO. 3 JUNCTION BLOCK) OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the combination meter    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal. (b) Reconnect the L27 connector to the combination meter assembly. (c) Reconnect the L5 connector to the power management control ECU. (d) Connect an oscilloscope to terminal L27-7 (+S) and body ground. (e) Turn the power switch on (IG). (f) Turn the wheel slowly. (g) Check the signal waveform according to the condition(s) in the table below. OK: The waveform is displayed as shown in the illustration. Text in Illustration HINT When the system is functioning normally, one wheel revolution generates 4 pulses. As the vehicle speed increases, the width indicated by (A) in the illustration narrows. NG --  GO TO METER / GAUGE SYSTEM (Speed Signal Circuit)  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Speed Signal Circuit OK --  REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY - NO. 3 JUNCTION BLOCK) (a) Disconnect the 3A connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER - NO. 3 JUNCTION BLOCK) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU - JUNCTION BLOCK) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU - NO. 3 JUNCTION BLOCK) OK --  REPLACE NO. 3 JUNCTION BLOCK

dtcs.description: Brake Signal Malfunction The power management control ECU receives brake signal information from 2 sources. It receives a signal from the stop light switch assembly via a direct line, and a signal from the skid control ECU assembly via CAN. If the information from these 2 sources is inconsistent, this DTC will be stored. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuse for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. HINT Check the connector connection to the terminal to make sure that there is no abnormality such as a loose connection, deformation, etc. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU)   See: B2271 NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU - BODY GROUND)   See: B2271 NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU - BODY GROUND) OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT STOP LIGHT SWITCH ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the stop light switch assembly    See: Lighting and Horns/Brake Light Switch/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE STOP LIGHT SWITCH ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Brake Light Switch/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STOP LIGHT SWITCH ASSEMBLY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) (a) Disconnect the A22 connector from the power management control ECU. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (STOP LIGHT SWITCH ASSEMBLY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) OK -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (STOP LIGHT SWITCH ASSEMBLY) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / Power Source Control / Data List. (e) Read the Data List according to the display on the Techstream. Power Source Control OK: ON (brake pedal is depressed) and OFF (brake pedal is released) appear on the screen. NG --  REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  GO TO ELECTRONICALLY CONTROLLED BRAKE SYSTEM (DTC C1249/49)  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

dtcs.description: Runnable Signal Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction is detected in the READY signal circuit inside the power management control ECU. NOTICE: * When the power management control ECU is replaced with a new one and the cable from the negative (-) auxiliary battery terminal is connected, the power source mode becomes on (IG) mode. When the auxiliary battery is removed and reinstalled, the power source mode that was selected when the auxiliary battery was removed is restored. * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. HINT Check the connector connection to the terminal to make sure that there is no abnormality such as a loose connection, deformation, etc. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU)   See: B2271 NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (AUXILIARY BATTERY - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU - BODY GROUND)   See: B2271 NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU - BODY GROUND) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Smart Key System (for Start Function). (b) Turn the power switch on (READY). (c) After 25 seconds have elapsed, check if the DTC has been set again. OK:

dtcs.description: LIN Communication Master Malfunction This DTC is stored when there is an open, short or ECU communication malfunction between the power management control ECU and certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly). NOTICE: * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, register the key. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs. HINT Check the both "Smart Key" and "Power Source Control" DTCs. Result: HINT When DTC B2287 and B2785 are output simultaneously, perform troubleshooting for DTC B2785 first. B --  GO TO DTC B2785  See: B2785 A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) (a) Disconnect the L6 power management control ECU and L62 certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU (a) Replace the power management control ECU    See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs. OK:

dtcs.description: can be stored in each power window regulator motor assembly (power window ECU). D-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Driver Side)) P-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Front Passenger Side)) RL-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Rear LH Side)) RR-Door Motor (Power Window Regulator Motor Assembly (for Rear RH Side)) NOTICE: * When the power window regulator motor assembly is reinstalled or replaced, the power window control system must be initialized. * After a door glass or a door glass run has been replaced, the jam protection function may operate unexpectedly when the auto up function is used. In such cases, the auto up function can be reinitialized by repeating the following operations at least 5 times: (1) Close the power window by fully pulling up the multiplex network master switch assembly or power window regulator switch assembly and holding it in the auto up position. (2) Open the power window by fully pushing down the switch. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (APPLICABLE LOCATION) (a) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (b) Turn the power switch on (IG). (c) Turn the Techstream on. (d) Enter the following menus: Body Electrical / (desired system) / Active Test. HINT Perform the Active Test for the power window regulator motor assembly that has DTC B2311 stored in its ECU. (e) Perform the Active Test according to the display on the Techstream.

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (APPLICABLE LOCATION) (a) Initialize the power window regulator motor assembly    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. HINT Initialize the power window regulator motor assembly that has DTC B2311 stored in its ECU. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK POWER WINDOW CONTROL SYSTEM (APPLICABLE LOCATION) (a) Check that the power window operates normally by opening and closing the window    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Operation Check. HINT Check the power window operation of the window where DTC B2311 has been stored. OK: Each power window operates normally. Result: E --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY (for Rear RH Side)  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Rear Door/Rear Door Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Removal D --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY (for Rear LH Side)  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Rear Door/Rear Door Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Removal C --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY (for Front Passenger Side)  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Removal B --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY (for Driver Side)  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK WHETHER PARTS HAVE BEEN INSTALLED CORRECTLY (a) Check that the power window components are installed correctly. OK: Power window components are installed correctly. HINT When the power window components are installed correctly, the problem has occurred because the battery is disconnected while the power switch is on (IG), the power window components are installed improperly or the motor is overheated. NG --  INSTALL PARTS CORRECTLY OK --  END

dtcs.description: Driver Side Door ECU Communication Stop This DTC is stored when LIN communication between the power window regulator motor assembly (for driver side) and main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) stops for 10 seconds or more. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * When a power window regulator motor assembly is replaced or removed and reinstalled, it requires initialization See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Rear Door LH ECU Communication Stop This DTC is stored when LIN communication between the power window regulator motor assembly (for rear LH side) and main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) stops for 10 seconds or more. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * When a power window regulator motor assembly is replaced or removed and reinstalled, it requires initialization See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: LIN Communication Bus Malfunction The main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) monitors communication between all the ECUs connected to the door bus lines. When the main body ECU (multiplex network body ECU) detects errors in communication with all the ECUs connected to the door bus lines at 2.6-second intervals and 3 times in a row, DTC B2325 will be stored. * *1: w/ Moonroof with Solar Panel NOTICE: * When the sliding roof ECU (sliding roof drive gear assembly) is replaced or removed and reinstalled, it requires initialization See: Body and Frame/Roof and Associated Components/Sunroof / Moonroof/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning . * When a power window regulator motor assembly is replaced or removed and reinstalled, it requires initialization See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result C --  INSPECT HEADLIGHT ASSEMBLY B --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (INTEGRATION RELAY - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (INTEGRATION RELAY - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the 1H integration relay connector. (b) Measure the voltage and resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration (c) Disconnect the 1G integration relay connector. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (INTEGRATION RELAY - INSTRUMENT PANEL JUNCTION BLOCK ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the 1G integration relay connector. (b) Disconnect the 2C instrument panel junction block assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT INSTRUMENT PANEL JUNCTION BLOCK ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the instrument panel junction block assembly. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL JUNCTION BLOCK ASSEMBLY OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE INTEGRATION NO.1 RELAY (a) Temporarily replace the integration relay with a new or normally functioning one    See: Lighting and Horns/Daytime Running Lamp/Daytime Running Lamp Relay/Service and Repair/Components. (b) Check for DTC    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

dtcs.description: Initialization has not been Performed The headlight leveling ECU assembly stores this DTC if initialization has not been performed after the ECU was replaced. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Exterior Lighting System/Initialization. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK FOR DTC (a) Using the Techstream, recheck for DTCs. Result: HINT If the headlight leveling ECU assembly stores DTC B2450 even though the ECU has been initialized, there may be a malfunction in the headlight leveling ECU assembly. B --  REPLACE HEADLIGHT LEVELING ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Headlamp/Headlamp Alignment Control Module/Service and Repair/Components A --  END

dtcs.description: Height Control Sensor Data Out of Range When Initializing The headlight leveling ECU assembly stores this DTC if the sensor value received from the height control sensor is out of range when performing initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly; for example, the vehicle is not level or being jacked up. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Perform initialization of the headlight leveling ECU assembly    See: Lighting and Horns/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Exterior Lighting System/Initialization. HINT * Before initializing, make sure that the vehicle condition is proper for initialization. * Be sure to perform the initialization as instructed. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK FOR DTC (a) Using the Techstream, recheck for DTCs. Result: HINT If the headlight leveling ECU assembly stores DTC B2452 even though the ECU has been initialized as instructed, there may be a malfunction in the headlight leveling ECU assembly. B --  REPLACE HEADLIGHT LEVELING ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Lighting and Horns/Headlamp/Headlamp Alignment Control Module/Service and Repair/Components A --  END

dtcs.description: Antenna Coil Open / Short This DTC is stored when there is an open or short in the transponder key coil (built into the power switch). NOTICE: * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, register all the keys and ECU communication ID See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobiliser System.*1 * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, register all the keys See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobiliser System.*2 * *1: w/o Automatic Light Control System * *2: w/ Automatic Light Control System PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. OK:

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - POWER SWITCH) (a) Disconnect the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) connector. (b) Disconnect the power switch connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE POWER SWITCH (a) Replace the power switch    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Start / Stop Switch/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. OK:

dtcs.description: Communication Malfunction between ECUs Connected by LIN The certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) monitors communication between all the ECUs connected to the certification bus lines. When the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) detects errors in communication with all the ECUs connected to the certification bus lines at a set interval and 3 times in a row, DTC B2785 will be stored. * *1: w/ Automatic Light Control System NOTICE: * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, register the key. * If the transmission control ECU assembly is replaced, register the ECU code. * If the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) is replaced, register the ECU code and ECU communication ID. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - EACH ECU) (a) Disconnect the L62, L6, L10*1 and A24 connectors. *1: w/ Automatic Light Control System (b) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: *1: w/ Automatic Light Control System Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY) (a) Reconnect the L62, L6 and L10*1 connectors. *1: w/ Automatic Light Control System (b) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Recheck for DTCs. Result: B --  REPLACE TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Relays and Modules - Transmission and Drivetrain/Relays and Modules - CVT/Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU) (a) Reconnect the A24 connector. (b) Disconnect the L6 connector. (c) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Recheck for DTCs. Result: C --  REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal B --  REPLACE CERTIFICATION ECU (SMART KEY ECU ASSEMBLY)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (ID CODE BOX (IMMOBILISER CODE ECU)) (a) Reconnect the L6 connector. (b) Disconnect the L10 connector. (c) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (d) Recheck for DTCs. Result: B --  REPLACE ID CODE BOX (IMMOBILISER CODE ECU)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Keyless Start Antenna/Service and Repair/Removal A --  REPLACE CERTIFICATION ECU (SMART KEY ECU ASSEMBLY)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: No Response from Steering Lock ECU This DTC is stored when LIN communication between the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) and transmission control ECU assembly stops for 10 seconds or more. HINT Although "No Response from Steering Lock ECU" is displayed on the Techstream, this message is shown if the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) cannot communicate with the transmission control ECU assembly. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, register the key. * If the transmission control ECU assembly is replaced, register the ECU code. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the A23 and A24 transmission control ECU assembly connectors. (b) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the L62 certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE TRANSMISSION CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the transmission control ECU assembly    See: Powertrain Management/Transmission Control Systems/Relays and Modules - Transmission and Drivetrain/Relays and Modules - CVT/Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs. OK:

dtcs.description: No Response from ID BOX This DTC is stored when LIN communication between the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) and ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) stops for 10 seconds or more. NOTICE: * Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, register the key. * If the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) is replaced, register the ECU code and ECU communication ID. * When using the Techstream to troubleshoot with the power switch off: Connect the Techstream to the DLC3, and turn the courtesy switch on and off at 1.5-second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ID CODE BOX - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the L10 ID code box connector. (b) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - ID CODE BOX) (a) Disconnect the L62 certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE ID CODE BOX (IMMOBILISER CODE ECU) (a) Replace the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Keyless Start Antenna/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTC    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs. OK:

dtcs.description: ID BOX EEPROM Malfunction This DTC is stored when the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) detects an internal malfunction. NOTICE: * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, register all the keys See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobiliser System. * If the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) is replaced, register the ECU code and ECU communication ID See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobiliser System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE ID CODE BOX (IMMOBILISER CODE ECU) (a) Replace the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Keyless Start Antenna/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

ECU CODE REGISTRATION (a) Register the ECU code    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobiliser System. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

ECU COMMUNICATION ID REGISTRATION (a) Register the ECU communication ID    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobiliser System. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. OK:

dtcs.description: Engine Immobiliser System Malfunction This DTC is stored when one of the following occurs: 1) the power management control ECU (HV CPU) detects an error in its own communication with the certification ECU assembly (smart key ECU assembly)*1 or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)*2; 2) the power management control ECU (HV CPU) detects an error in the communication lines; or 3) the ECU communication ID between the certification ECU assembly (smart key ECU assembly)*1 or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)*2 and power management control ECU (HV CPU) is different and a hybrid vehicle control system start is attempted. * *1: w/o Automatic Light Control System * *2: w/ Automatic Light Control System HINT Before troubleshooting this DTC, make sure that no certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) DTCs are present. If present, troubleshoot the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) DTCs first. * *1: w/o Automatic Light Control System * *2: w/ Automatic Light Control System

steps.instruction: w/o Automatic Light Control System

w/ Automatic Light Control System NOTICE: * If the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) is replaced, register all the keys and ECU communication ID    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobiliser System.*1 * If the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) is replaced, register the ECU code and ECU communication ID    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Engine Immobiliser System.*2 * *1: w/o Automatic Light Control System * *2: w/ Automatic Light Control System PROCEDURE

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. OK:

CHECK CONNECTOR CONNECTION CONDITION (a) Turn the power switch off. (b) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the power management control ECU (HV CPU) and certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly)*1 or ID code box (immobiliser code ECU)*2. *1: w/o Automatic Light Control System *2: w/ Automatic Light Control System OK: Connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTORS PROPERLY OK -- Continue to next step.
SYSTEM CHECK (a) Check the vehicle specification. Result: B --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ID CODE BOX - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU (HV CPU)) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU (HV CPU)) (a) Disconnect the certification ECU (smart key ECU assembly) connector. (b) Disconnect the power management control ECU (HV CPU) connector. (c) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU (HV CPU) (a) Replace the power management control ECU (HV CPU)    See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. OK:

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (ID CODE BOX - POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU (HV CPU)) (a) Disconnect the ID code box (immobiliser code ECU) connector. (b) Disconnect the power management control ECU (HV CPU) connector. (c) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE POWER MANAGEMENT CONTROL ECU (HV CPU) (a) Replace the power management control ECU (HV CPU)    See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Module/Service and Repair/Removal. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC OUTPUT (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. (b) Recheck for DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Engine Immobiliser System. OK:
